body.title: Web dashboard: show all devices, health details, OR spend, and uptime
body.result: Added /api/rack/health JSON endpoint + JS-driven /rack page: device list from flat-file registry with expandable detail, OR budget, machines with device grouping, 10s auto-refresh
body.description:
The rack dashboard at /rack currently shows machines (online/offline) but not rack devices or their health detail. For external users on Monday this is the first thing they see — it needs to show the full picture.

Required additions:
1. Rack devices list — every registered device with: name, status (healthy/degraded/offline), last_seen, device_type
2. Per-device health detail — expand each device to show its health() response (the structured DeviceHealth fields, not just online/offline)
3. OpenRouter spend — already present, keep it; add day/week/month breakdown once T-inference-budget-ledger ships (gate on that ticket)
4. Uptime — already present, keep it
5. Machine → device grouping — show which devices are running on which machine

**Affected files:** devices/web_server/routes.py (or equivalent), devices/web_server/templates/ or static/, skeleton/registry (health rollup reads from here)
**Design rules:** /api/health already aggregates health — web dashboard should consume that endpoint, not re-query devices directly
**Scope boundary:** IN — device list, per-device health detail, machine grouping, OR spend breakdown; OUT — real-time streaming updates (polling is fine), historical charts
**Completion criteria:** /rack shows all registered devices with health status; clicking/expanding a device shows its full health() response; OR spend visible; a new developer loading /rack gets a complete picture of what's running and what's healthy
